qas.q: MI Corps March
qas.a:
Onward to victory.
Our silent warriors to the fight.
Onward to victory.
Trained and ready day or night.
Peace through intelligence.
Here's to you health and to our corps.
Strength through intelligence.
Tojours Avant forever more.

qas.q: Military Intelligence (MI) Creed
qas.a:
I am a soldier first, but an intelligence professional, second to none.

With pride in my heritage, but focused on the future. Performing the first task on an Army: To find, know, and never lose the enemy.

With a sense of urgency and of tenacity, professional and physical fitness, and above all: Integrity - for in truth lies victory.

Always at silent war while ready for a shooting war: The Silent Warrior of the Army Team.

qas.q: The Army Song
qas.a:
March along, sing our song, with the Army of the free.
Count the brave, count the true, who have fought to victory.
We're the Army and proud of our name!
We're the Army and proudly proclaim:

First to fight for the right,
And to build the Nation's might,
And the Army goes rolling along.

Proud of all we have done,
Fighting till the battle's won,
And the Army goes rolling along.

Then it's hi! hi! hey!
The Army's on its way.
Count off the cadence loud and strong;
For where'er we go,
You will always know
That the Army goes rolling along.

qas.q: The Soldier's Creed
qas.a:
I am an American Soldier.
I am a warrior and a member of a team.
I serve the people of the United States, and live the Army Values.
I will always place the mission first.
I will never accept defeat.
I will never quit.
I will never leave a fallen comrade.
I am disciplined, physically and mentally tough, trained and proficient in my warrior tasks and drills.
I always maintain my arms, my equipment and myself.
I am an expert and I am a professional.
I stand ready to deploy, engage, and destroy, the enemies of the United States of America in close combat.
I am a guardian of freedom and the American way of life.
I am an American Soldier.

qas.q: Army Values
qas.a:
Loyalty: Bear true faith and allegiance to the U.S. Constitution, the Army, your unit, and other soldiers.
Duty: Fulfill your obligations.
Respect: Treat other people as they should be treated.
Selfless Service: Put the welfare of the nation, the Army, and your subordinates before your own.
Honor: Live up to all the Army values.
Integrity: Do what's right, legally and morally.
Personal Courage: Face fear, danger, or adversity (physical or moral).

⁃ Courtesies: written down and is adhered to

Company: Raptors
Battalion: Standing Guard
Military Intel: Always Out Front
Army: This We'll Defend

qas.q: General Orders
qas.a:
1. I will guard everything within the limits of my post and quit my post only when properly relieved.
2. I will obey my special orders and perform all my duties in a military manner.
3. I will report violations of my special orders, emergencies, and any thing not covered in my instructions to the commander of the relief.

qas.q: SHARP
3 Categories of Sexual Harassment
2 Types of Sexual Harassment
qas.a:
⁃ Quid pro quo: this for that
⁃ Hostile Environment: occurs when Soldiers or civilians are subjected to unwanted comments or behaviors

qas.q: 5 Techniques of Dealing with Sexual Harassment
qas.a:
⁃ Direct approach
⁃ Indirect approach
⁃ Third party
⁃ Chain of command
⁃ Filing a formal complaint

qas.q: Sexual Harassment Reporting Options
qas.a:
⁃ Formal: complaint is filed by submitting a sworn statement on an equal opportunity form within 60 calendar days of the alleged incident. the complainant will receive written findings.
⁃ Informal: any complaint an individual does not wish to put in writing. in some cases this type of complaint may be resolved while maintaining confidentiality of the complaint. the complainant and the va develop a plan of action to address the sexual harassment. confidentiality will never be promised or guaranteed to the complainant.

qas.q: Sexual Assault Reporting Options
qas.a:
⁃ Restricted: allows soldier and adult family members to disclose details of sexual assault to specifically identified individuals and receive medical treatment and counseling, without initiating an official investigation.
⁃ Unrestricted: allows victim who desire medical treatment, counseling, and an official investigation of the allegation to report the incident to the sarc/sharp or va/sharp specialist, chain of command or law enforcement.

qas.a:

⁃ Informal: not filed in writing and no investigation is launched

qas.q: When are you allowed to wear the Tan T-shirt as an outer garment?
qas.a: When CO authorizes due to extreme heat.

qas.q: On what shoulder is the PT belt worn when in the IPFU jacket?
qas.a: Over the right shoulder diagonally from the right shoulder to the left side of the waist.
